skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Execute implementation plans in small batches with review checkpoints, verification steps, and clear stopping rules.
skill.summary: Run implementation plans through controlled batches with checkpoint reviews.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 17 external command findings, but they are Markdown fences and a documented npm test example, not executable skill code. The weak cryptography findings on SKILL.md line 3 are false positives from the description text. No prompt injection, exfiltration, hidden code, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown examples and workflow templates. They do not execute by themselves, but the skill can guide agents to run verification commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are fenced Markdown blocks, including a template and one npm test example. There is no Ruby, shell backtick execution, or executable script file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a Text Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to the description line. That line describes review checkpoints and contains no cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md line 3 is plain metadata text. I found no DES, MD5, SHA1, encryption code, or password hashing logic there.

content.value_statement: Large implementation plans can drift when tasks are handled without checkpoints. This skill keeps execution organized through small batches, verification, reporting, and feedback pauses.
actual_capabilities: Loads and reviews a complete implementation plan before execution.
Runs work in default batches of three tasks.
Marks task status as in progress and completed.
Runs the verification steps specified by the plan.
Reports batch results and waits for architect feedback.
Stops when blockers, unclear instructions, or critical plan gaps appear.
limitations: Requires a complete implementation plan before use.
Does not create the plan itself.
Depends on available project tools for tests and verification.

use_cases.title: Controlled Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Execute a prepared feature plan in manageable groups with status tracking and verification after each task.

use_cases.title: Architect Review Workflow
use_cases.description: Pause after each batch so an architect can review progress before the next tasks begin.

use_cases.title: Structured Refactor Execution
use_cases.description: Apply a refactor plan step by step while preserving tests, build checks, and clear reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin executing a completed implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use executing-plans on this implementation plan. Review it first, then start the first batch only.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Smaller Batch
prompt_templates.scenario: Limit execution when the plan has risky changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use executing-plans with a batch size of two tasks. Stop after the batch report and wait for feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Continue After Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Resume after an architect has reviewed the previous batch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this feedback to the completed batch, then continue executing the next batch from the plan.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Blockers Strictly
prompt_templates.scenario: Execute a complex plan with strict stop conditions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use executing-plans for this plan. Stop immediately on failed verification, missing dependency, unclear instruction, or critical design gap.

output_examples.input: Execute the first three tasks in the plan and report status.
output_examples.output: Batch 1 completed. Task 1 passed tests. Task 2 passed build checks. Task 3 passed lint. Awaiting architect feedback.

output_examples.input: Continue after this review note and run the next batch.
output_examples.output: Feedback applied. Batch 2 started with Task 4 in progress. Verification will run after each task as specified.

output_examples.input: Run the plan until you hit a blocker.
output_examples.output: Execution stopped before Task 5. The plan requires a missing dependency. Clarification is needed before continuing.

best_practices: Provide a complete plan with clear task boundaries and verification commands.
Review each batch report before asking the assistant to continue.
Keep batch size small when tasks affect shared architecture.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to invent requirements or fill major plan gaps.
Do not continue after failed verification without explicit approval.
Do not skip the feedback pause between batches.

faq.question: Does this skill write implementation plans?
faq.answer: No. It executes an existing plan. Use a planning workflow before this skill.

faq.question: What is the default batch size?
faq.answer: The default batch size is three tasks.

faq.question: Can I change the batch size?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for a smaller or larger batch size before execution starts.

faq.question: When does the skill stop?
faq.answer: It stops for blockers, unclear instructions, critical plan gaps, or required feedback checkpoints.

faq.question: Does it run tests?
faq.answer: It runs the verification steps listed in the plan when the project tools are available.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
